objective: Stabilize the Windows no-focus clean-install baselines so transient hosted throttling is classified separately from plugin regressions.

notes: Focused runner tests passed. Live Windows managed-baseline passed with a successful hosted turn and one recovery transient recorded under transientFailures. Live Windows provider-connected baseline passed with OpenRouter gpt-5.4-mini and recovered to the managed SystemSculpt model cleanly.

Verified the new autoresearch harness itself. ./autoresearch.sh passed end to end on the real Windows VM, including a managed-baseline run that recovered from a transient hosted failure by succeeding on the managed recovery turn and a provider-connected OpenRouter gpt-5.4-mini pass that recovered back to the managed SystemSculpt model.

objective: Stabilize the Windows no-focus clean-install baselines through a single combined baselines command so transient hosted throttling stays classified separately from plugin regressions.

notes: Introduced the combined baselines case and package scripts so managed and provider-connected parity now run under one attach and one report surface. Local runner tests passed, npm run test:native:windows:baselines passed on the real Windows VM, and ./autoresearch.sh passed end to end with a pinned OpenRouter gpt-5.4-mini provider turn before recovering back to the managed SystemSculpt model.

Added a first-class baselineSummary surface to the desktop runner and switched ./autoresearch.sh to consume it directly. The updated runner suite passed locally with 23 tests, and the real Windows combined baselines run stayed green with the concise summary line showing overall=ok, provider=openrouter, recovery=ok, and transientFailures=0.

objective: Stabilize ChatView local Pi tool-calling so Pi-executed tools are not re-executed as hosted tool requests, and extend the provider-connected baseline to exercise real ChatView filesystem tool prompts on local-pi-openrouter@@openai/gpt-5.4-mini for macOS and Windows.

Stabilize ChatView local Pi tool-calling so Pi-executed tools stay completed locally instead of being re-executed as hosted tool requests, while keeping the provider-connected OpenRouter GPT-5.4 Mini filesystem tool baseline green on macOS and Windows through the canonical bundled auth-storage path.

notes: Removed the runtime-relative createBundledPiAuthStorage fallback from StudioPiAuthInventory and imported the bundled auth storage directly so the built plugin can no longer silently hide broken provider auth reads. Targeted auth/runtime preflight stayed green (studio-pi-auth-storage-fetch-shim, PiSdkRuntime.paths, settings-providers-tab.import-safe), npm run build passed, bash autoresearch.sh passed with failing_checks=0, Windows provider-connected OpenRouter gpt-5.4-mini now flips provider state from none to api_key and completes a real filesystem tool turn with 3/3 relevant tool calls completed, and macOS passed the same provider-connected filesystem tool turn on local-pi-openrouter@@openai/gpt-5.4-mini.
